app.title: Word Hunter
app.tagline: App to track new words to learn
app.footer: Data is stored in this browser.
app.description: Word Hunter — local Rust/Tauri reader, vocabulary trainer, and offline translator.

topbar.newTooltip: Words added in the current session

onboarding.languageCopy: Pick the app language and the language profile you want to learn first. You can change both later in Settings.

library.heading: Books
library.empty: No books match the current filter.
library.removeConfirmMessage: Remove “{title}” from your library? This cannot be undone.

import.heading: Import
import.fileHint: UTF-8 text files, subtitle files .srt, .vtt, .ass, .epub, and PDFs with a text layer or desktop OCR are supported. MOBI/AZW works when Calibre (ebook-convert) is installed. Scanned PDF OCR uses the bundled native PaddleOCR runtime.
import.mobileFileHint: On Android, import accepts EPUB, lightweight UTF-8 text files, subtitle files .srt, .vtt, .ass, and PDFs with a text layer. Move scanned PDFs and MOBI/AZW from PC through sync.
import.youtubeChooseTrack: Found {count} subtitle tracks. Choose one and import.
import.youtubeNoTracks: No captions found for this video.
import.title: Title
import.author: Author (optional)
import.textPlaceholder: Paste text or load a file above...
import.ocrTakingLonger: Taking longer than estimated ({elapsed}) — still working…
import.ocrWholeBookConfirm: OCR will process the whole book. Large PDFs may take many hours — keep the app open.
import.ocrWholeBookStatus: Processing the whole book… {elapsed} elapsed. This may take a long time; keep the app open.
import.pdfTextLayerBlurb: Imported from a PDF text layer ({pages} pages). Scanned PDFs need OCR in the desktop app.

reader.title: Reader
reader.wordPanelHeading: Click a word in the reader
reader.wordPanelHint: The selected word will appear here with a note, status and example sentence.
reader.loadingHint: Loading text. This can take a few seconds...

vocab.heading: Vocabulary list
vocab.reviewEmptyHint: Add words from the reader or change the status of existing entries.

discover.heading: Book search
discover.sortHint: “Author year” is the author's birth year — Gutendex doesn't expose publication year.
discover.levelHint: Heuristic: filters Project Gutenberg by topic
discover.selectionHint: Tick books to add several at once.
discover.emptyPrompt: Enter a phrase and click “Search” to query the Project Gutenberg catalog.
discover.infoBody: Search uses the open gutendex.com API. Once added, metadata is stored locally and the text is fetched from Project Gutenberg on demand.
discover.error: Could not fetch results. Try again.
discover.noResults: No results for the given phrase.
discover.statsNoText: No text available in Project Gutenberg

settings.learningHint: Changing the language switches the active vocabulary and library database.
settings.cardStatsHint: Shows each book's reading progress and vocabulary count in the Library cards.
settings.coversHint: Shows book cover thumbnails when Gutenberg metadata provides one; books without covers stay text-only.
settings.removalBehaviorHint: Ignore keeps the entry and hides it from learning. Delete removes it completely, so the word appears as New again.
settings.autoTtsOnWordFocusHint: Plays pronunciation whenever word focus changes by click or keyboard navigation.
settings.ttsWordHighlightHint: Shows a yellow outline on the word currently being read when the TTS engine reports word timing.
settings.touchControlsHint: On desktop, makes buttons and spacing roomier for touch screens, laptops, and tablets.
settings.readerFocusModeHint: On desktop, hides the top bar, book metadata, and shortcut hints to leave more room for text.
settings.readerWordPanelVisibleHint: Lets you hide the right panel and read at full width when you do not need it.
settings.highlightHint: Underlines saved words in the reader using their learning-status colors. Turn off for plain text.
settings.autoLearnHint: First click on an unknown word immediately saves it as Learning instead of leaving it New.
settings.dataFolderHint: Choose where WordHunter keeps its local data. Keep this folder on the device for the safest startup.
settings.dataFolderSteps: WordHunter can prepare its own sync folder and keep it updated from the local app data.
settings.dataFolderCloudDelay: Cloud copies should mirror that WordHunter folder. Direct cloud mounts can be delayed or incomplete, so keep files available offline before using another device.
settings.dataFolderConfirm:
Choose where WordHunter stores local data.

The safest option is a folder on this device. Choose a cloud folder with the separate sync button.

Continue?
settings.cloudSyncUnavailable: Cloud sync is not available yet. Check the connector status.
settings.androidDataFolderFixed: On Android, local data stays in the protected app folder. Choose a separate sync folder instead.
settings.syncStatusDefault: Sync status: local app data only.
settings.syncStatusReady: Sync status: folder selected. Wait until cloud sync finishes before opening another device.
settings.syncStatusError: Sync status: save failed. Check folder access and cloud sync.
settings.syncHealthCaution: Sync folder health: available, but this looks like a mounted or cloud-backed folder.
settings.syncHealthNeedsAttention: Sync folder health: {issues} file issues need review.
settings.syncHealthReadOnly: Sync folder health: WordHunter cannot write to this folder.
settings.syncHealthMissing: Sync folder health: configured folder is missing.
settings.syncHealthNotFolder: Sync folder health: configured path is not a folder.
settings.cloudSyncStatusComplete: Cloud sync: last sync completed for {remote}.
settings.cloudSyncStatusNotSupported: Cloud sync: not available on this platform yet.
settings.cloudSyncStatusAuthRequired: Cloud sync: sign in again with Connect Google Drive.
settings.cloudSyncStatusError: Cloud sync: failed. Check connector status.
settings.syncConflictRefresh: Run sync again to load conflict details.
settings.recoveryPendingSaveTemp: Interrupted save temp file is present.
settings.recoveryQuarantinedJournal: A corrupt interrupted-save journal was quarantined.
settings.migrationComplete: Storage migration complete; legacy data preserved.
settings.clearWordsHint: Removes all saved words, translations, and learning progress for this language.
settings.clearLibraryHint: Removes all custom texts and resets the book list while keeping your word database.
settings.dynamicLearningColors: Learning word color by SRS level
settings.dynamicLearningColorsHint: Level 1 starts orange and level 5 ends green. Adjust the colors below.
settings.inTextReviewHint: For Learning words, guess the translation first, then rate your recall from 1 to 5. (Reader review becomes available no sooner than the following day after first learning the word.)
settings.hideKnownIgnoredHint: Leaves known and ignored words unmarked, so only words that still need attention stand out.
settings.wordAlgorithmHint: New detects languages without spaces and short subtitle lines better, for example Japanese or Chinese. Classic splits only letter runs, so it can be more predictable for simple texts.
settings.srsAlgorithmHint: SM-2 schedules from your last grade and ease factor. FSRS estimates stability and difficulty from review history, so due dates can change more aggressively.
settings.backupHint: JSON contains settings, language profiles, vocabulary, custom texts, and hidden books. Importing JSON replaces the current app state with data from the file.
settings.ankiHint: TSV exports only words, translations, and the first context. Importing TSV adds or updates vocabulary, but does not transfer settings, books, or profiles.
settings.resetHint: Resets theme, reader, dictionary, TTS, and other preferences to defaults. It does not delete words or texts.
settings.clearHint: Clears the local app database: vocabulary, custom texts, settings, and progress. Export JSON first if you want a backup.
settings.autoAddLearningOnlyHint: Only Learning words enter the review queue. New words stay out until you mark them Learning.
settings.autoTranslateHint: When a saved word has an empty translation, fills it with the selected translation engine. Manual translations stay untouched.
settings.useEdgeTtsHint: Generates more natural and fluid speech. Requires internet and sends the read text to Microsoft servers.
settings.offlineTranslatorHint: Enables local CTranslate2 translation for words and full sentences. Works offline after downloading language packs (~100-200MB each).
settings.argosAsDictHint: Makes the dictionary button (M) open the built-in offline translator for the selected word instead of your dictionary URL.
settings.argosDownloadHint: Downloads local translation packages for the selected languages, including pairs with English and your learning language when available.
settings.argosDownloadWarning: Note: downloading models will take a while. Do not close the app during the process.
settings.ankiExportStatusesHint: Choose which word statuses are included in the TSV. Anki receives only word, translation, and context columns.

translator.sourcePlaceholder: Type a word or a full sentence...
translator.noModels: No models — download them in Settings
translator.title: Offline Translator
translator.placeholder: Type a word or a full sentence...
translator.footer: Powered locally by the offline translator

graphs.heading: Charts
graphs.empty: No vocabulary data yet. Start reading to build your word database.
graphs.dayOfWeek: Review Activity by Day of Week
graphs.noFsrsData: No FSRS data available — switch to FSRS scheduler in Settings

help.heading: Help and shortcuts
help.tip1: Importing: Desktop accepts UTF-8 text, subtitles, EPUB, PDFs, and MOBI/AZW through Calibre. PDFs with a text layer keep the page image under selectable words; scanned PDF OCR runs in the desktop app through PaddleOCR. Word Hunter Pocket on Android imports EPUB, lightweight text, subtitles, and text-layer PDFs.
help.tip2: Profiles: Changing the target language in Settings creates a completely separate vocabulary list and library.
help.tip3: Dictionary: You can set a custom dictionary URL in Settings. Use {{word}} as a placeholder.
help.tip4: Sync and backup: On Android, local data stays in the app folder. Choose a separate sync folder in Settings to share books and words with desktop. Use JSON export as a manual full backup.
help.tip5:
Status counters:
☆ (purple) — words added in the current session.
Gray number — all words stored in your database.
✎ (blue) — words you are currently learning.
✓ (green) — words fully learned (known).
help.tip6: Graphs tab: G shows vocabulary progress, review forecasts, status distribution, intervals, ease, daily activity, and heatmaps.
help.tip7: Flashcard chart: In Settings you choose whether flashcards show a heatmap, forecast, intervals, ease, or repetitions.
help.tip8: PDF reader: Use the PDF zoom controls, mouse wheel, or pinch gesture on Android to inspect page images. The imported PDF text layer is stored with the book, while vocabulary, progress, sync, and backups keep the same local-first data model.
help.creditGutenberg: Project Gutenberg / Gutendex — free public domain book library and metadata search API.
help.creditWikipedia: Wikipedia API — free and fast image search for flashcards in the target learning language.
help.creditYouglish: YouGlish — contextual pronunciation video search widget using the YouGlish API.
help.creditSrs: SM-2 & FSRS — SuperMemo-2 and Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler algorithms for flashcard scheduling.
help.creditSqlite: SQLite & JSON — local storage for book contents, vocabulary, preferences, and progress.
help.creditPyside: Tauri 2 + Rust — native app shell. Desktop uses the system WebView/WebView2, while Word Hunter Pocket on Android runs in Android System WebView with the Rust backend compiled as a native library.
help.creditInter: Inter font — designed by Rasmus Andersson (SIL Open Font License 1.1). Used as the primary UI typeface.
help.contactLicense: License: AGPL-3.0-or-later. Closed-source commercial use of derivative code requires a separate written license. Details: https://github.com/Ironship/WordHunter/blob/master/LICENSE
help.whatsNew: What's new in 0.3.6.2: follow-up hotfixes add managed Google Drive sync with staged cloud pulls and retryable authorization, make Discover follow the active learning profile language while preserving the selected source, and improve Linux/Flatpak window control clicks.

readerKeys.font: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>+</kbd> / <kbd>-</kbd> - reader text size; <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Alt</kbd> + <kbd>+</kbd>/<kbd>-</kbd>/<kbd>0</kbd> - desktop interface scale
readerKeys.page: <kbd>PgUp</kbd> / <kbd>PgDn</kbd> - change page; <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>G</kbd> - focus the page number field
readerKeys.imgSuggest: <kbd>I</kbd> - Search image, <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>1</kbd>/<kbd>2</kbd>/<kbd>3</kbd> - Select suggestion, <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>4</kbd> - Upload own image
readerKeys.inTextReview: <kbd>Enter</kbd> - Show the answer during an in-text review, <kbd>1</kbd>–<kbd>5</kbd> - Rate the answer

actionKeys.merge: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + Click - Merge selected word with newly clicked word
actionKeys.status: <kbd>1</kbd> <kbd>2</kbd> <kbd>3</kbd> <kbd>4</kbd> - Change status: New / Learning / Known / Ignored
actionKeys.editFields: <kbd>E</kbd> - Focus translation field, <kbd>N</kbd> - focus note field, <kbd>I</kbd> - search image

help.sm2Body1: Flashcards use FSRS by default, tracking each word's stability and difficulty. You can switch to classic SM-2 in Settings.
help.sm2BodyFsrs: FSRS: ratings update card stability and difficulty, so due dates can adapt more strongly to your review history than SM-2.
help.sm2Grade02: 0-2 (Forgot): The interval resets to 1 day and the word will appear more frequently.
help.sm2Grade3: 3 (Hard): Correct, but the interval grows slowly.
help.sm2Grade5: 5 (Easy): Perfect recall, the interval grows much faster.
help.creditOfflineTranslator: Translation and OCR — the desktop app can use CTranslate2 / ctranslate2-rs, SentencePiece, and PaddleOCR. Heavy model packages are external assets with their own licenses; Android favors lighter features, system TTS, and data sync.
